steps.title: Identify the organizing principle
steps.content: The passage opens with a single definitive claim: silversword species "collectively illustrate the process of adaptive radiation." Every detail that follows — physical diversity, common ancestry, habitat adaptation — supports this central idea. The main idea must capture that silverswords are examples of adaptive radiation.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Check each option against the passage's central claim:
  • Choice A — ‘All silverswords that grow on the Hawaiian archipelago have similar physical characteristics.’: The passage explicitly states each species is "physically distinct" — this directly contradicts the text. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The Hawaiian archipelago exhibits many distinct habitats and species.’: The archipelago's habitats are mentioned only as context for silversword adaptation, not as the main idea. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Most plants that grow on the Hawaiian archipelago are descendants of a single founder species.’: The passage discusses only silverswords, not most plants on the archipelago — this overgeneralizes. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Silverswords are good examples of adaptive radiation on the Hawaiian archipelago.’: This restates the passage's opening claim directly, and all details in the passage support it. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
